title: Authors and Citation
text: Sehyun Oh. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Oh S (2026). CellGraphR: Graph Analysis Histopathology Images. R package version 0.99.0, https://shbrief.github.io/CellGraphR.
code: @Manual{,   title = {CellGraphR: Graph Analysis for Histopathology Images},   author = {Sehyun Oh},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.99.0},   url = {https://shbrief.github.io/CellGraphR}, }

title: CellGraphR
text: CellGraphR R/Bioconductor package importing, analysing, visualising cell-level spatial graphs derived whole-slide histopathology images. bridges Python cell-graph construction pipeline Bioconductor analysis ecosystem, providing unified workflow raw graph data clinical association testing.

title: CellGraphR
text: Import pre-computed PyTables HDF5 cell-graph files igraph objects Construct spatial graphs (Delaunay, k-NN, Gabriel, relative neighbourhood, MST) cell coordinates Extract graph-topology features (transitivity, path length, motifs, spectral metrics) spatial statistics (Moran’s , Ripley’s K/L, neighbourhood composition, Hopkins clustering) Store patient-level feature matrices SummarizedExperiment integrate MultiAssayExperiment Visualise spatial graphs overlaid tissue images ggplot2 Test associations MOFA latent factors clinical variables

title: CellGraphR
text: Install development version GitHub:

title: CellGraphR
text: CellGraphR uses reticulate read HDF5 graph files produced Python pipeline. following Python packages required: Python >= 3.8 pandas >= 1.3 tables >= 3.6 (PyTables)

title: CellGraphR
code: library(CellGraphR)  # --- 1. Import a cell graph from an HDF5 file --- h5_path <- system.file("extdata", "TCGA-EJ-5494_graph.h5", package = "CellGraphR") g <- importGraphH5(h5_path)  # --- 2. Build a spatial graph from nuclei coordinates --- sg <- buildSpatialGraph(nuclei_list, graph_type = "delaunay")  # --- 3. Visualise the graph --- plotGraph(g)  # --- 4. Extract graph features --- topo_feats <- extract_graph_features(nuclei_list) spatial_feats <- extract_advanced_spatial_features(nuclei_list)  # --- 5. Import features into a SummarizedExperiment --- csv_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_graph_features.csv", package = "CellGraphR") se <- importGraphFeatures(csv_path)  # --- 6. Test factor–clinical associations --- results <- testFactorAssociations(factor_scores, clinical_df)

Add graph features to a MultiAssayExperiment — addGraphToMAE
text: mae MultiAssayExperiment. graph_se SummarizedExperiment produced importGraphFeatures, patient IDs column names. experiment_name Character(1). Name new experiment slot returned MultiAssayExperiment. Default "graph_features".

title: Add graph features to a MultiAssayExperiment — addGraphToMAE

title: Add graph features to a MultiAssayExperiment — addGraphToMAE
text: patients present colData(mae) retained graph_se. sampleMap constructed automatically, assuming column names graph_se correspond directly primary sample identifiers mae.

title: Add graph features to a MultiAssayExperiment — addGraphToMAE
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(MultiAssayExperiment) # mae <- curatedTCGAData::curatedTCGAData("PRAD", "RNASeq2GeneNorm*", ...) # graph_se <- importGraphFeatures("path/to/features.csv") mae2 <- addGraphToMAE(mae, graph_se) experiments(mae2) } # }

what: Build a spatial igraph from a nuclei list — buildSpatialGraph
title: Build a spatial igraph from a nuclei list — buildSpatialGraph
text: Constructs one five spatial graph types (Delaunay, k-NN, MST, Gabriel, RNG) list nucleus objects returns igraph x, y, type vertex attributes.  resulting object can passed directly plotGraph.

title: Build a spatial igraph from a nuclei list — buildSpatialGraph
Build a spatial igraph from a nuclei list — buildSpatialGraph
text: nuclei_list list nucleus objects. element must contain: centroid Numeric vector length 2 (x, y coordinates). type Character scalar — cell-type label. type Character(1). Graph type build. One "delaunay", "knn", "mst", "gabriel", "rng". k Integer(1). Number nearest neighbours type = "knn". Ignored types. Default 5L.

title: Build a spatial igraph from a nuclei list — buildSpatialGraph
text: undirected igraph vertex attributes x, y (centroid coordinates) type (cell-type label).

title: Build a spatial igraph from a nuclei list — buildSpatialGraph
code: set.seed(42) nuclei <- lapply(seq_len(30), function(i)     list(centroid = runif(2, 0, 500),          type     = sample(c("neoplastic", "stromal", "immune"), 1)))  g_del <- buildSpatialGraph(nuclei, type = "delaunay") g_knn <- buildSpatialGraph(nuclei, type = "knn", k = 5) g_mst <- buildSpatialGraph(nuclei, type = "mst")

what: Classify graph feature names into thematic groups — .classify_graph_features
title: Classify graph feature names into thematic groups — .classify_graph_features
text: Assigns feature name one 14 biologically motivated thematic groups using regex pattern matching. Called automatically importGraphFeatures feature_groups = TRUE.

title: Classify graph feature names into thematic groups — .classify_graph_features
Classify graph feature names into thematic groups — .classify_graph_features
text: feature_names Character vector feature names.

title: Classify graph feature names into thematic groups — .classify_graph_features
text: Character vector group labels, length feature_names. Features match pattern labelled "".

title: Classify graph feature names into thematic groups — .classify_graph_features
text: 14 groups naming patterns trigger : global_topology n_cells, n_edges, mean/std/max/skewness/kurtosis degree. cell_type_composition frac_* — cell-type fractions. cell_type_mixing edge_frac_* — fraction edges cell types. neighborhood_radius mean_deg_rN, mean_mixrN, n_cellsrN — multi-scale (r20/40/80/100) features. per_celltype_graphmean_deg, mean_mix_, std_deg_, std_mix_* without radius suffix. cell_type_indicator is_* — binary presence flags. local_node degree, mixing_index, local_frac_. voronoivoronoi_ — Voronoi tessellation metrics. nearest_neighbor_dist nn_dist_* — inter-cell-type nearest-neighbor distances. clustering clustering_coeff_* — local clustering coefficients. spatial_autocorrelation morans_I_* — Moran's statistics. tumor_architecture tumor_, invasion_front, interior_tumor, neoplastic_ecc/hull. immune_spatialimmune_, TIL_* — immune infiltration metrics. network_community modularity_, n_connected_, celltype_entropy, local_entropy. network_motif total_triangles, homotypic_triangles, heterotypic_triangles, homotypic_triangle_frac. spatial_distribution features ending _dispersion _ratio (otherwise classified).

what: Extract advanced spatial relationship features — extract_advanced_spatial_features
title: Extract advanced spatial relationship features — extract_advanced_spatial_features
text: Computes comprehensive set spatial point-pattern statistics list nucleus objects, including spatial autocorrelation (Moran's ), multi-scale local density diversity, neighborhood composition, Hopkins clustering tendency, Ripley's K/L functions, nearest-neighbor statistics.

title: Extract advanced spatial relationship features — extract_advanced_spatial_features
Extract advanced spatial relationship features — extract_advanced_spatial_features
text: nuclei_list list nucleus objects. element must minimum: centroid (numeric vector length 2, x/y coordinates) type (character, cell-type label). image_dims Numeric vector c(width, height) specifying observation window pixels. verbose Logical. Print progress messages.

title: Extract advanced spatial relationship features — extract_advanced_spatial_features
text: named list elements: spatial_autocorr Moran's interpretation. multiscale Local density diversity scales 25, 50, 100, 200 pixels. neighborhood k-NN homogeneity diversity k = 3, 5, 10, 15. clustering Hopkins statistic clustering interpretation. ripley_k Ripley K/L function deviation statistics. nearest_neighbor Clark-Evans index k-NN distance summaries.

title: Extract advanced spatial relationship features — extract_advanced_spatial_features
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ feats <- extract_advanced_spatial_features(nuclei_list, image_dims = c(1024, 1024)) feats$spatial_autocorr$morans_i feats$nearest_neighbor$clark_evans_index } # }

what: Extract graph-based tissue organization features — extract_graph_features
title: Extract graph-based tissue organization features — extract_graph_features
text: Computes multiple graph representations cell spatial organization list nucleus objects returns nested list graph-topology metrics. Five graph types computed: Delaunay triangulation, k-nearest-neighbor (k = 3, 5, 7, 10), minimum spanning tree, Gabriel graph, relative neighborhood graph.

title: Extract graph-based tissue organization features — extract_graph_features
Extract graph-based tissue organization features — extract_graph_features
text: nuclei_list list nucleus objects. element must minimum: centroid (numeric vector length 2, x/y coordinates) type (character, cell-type label). image_dims Numeric vector c(width, height) specifying image dimensions pixels. Currently unused graph functions included API consistency extract_advanced_spatial_features. verbose Logical. Print progress messages.

title: Extract graph-based tissue organization features — extract_graph_features
text: named list elements: delaunay Graph metrics Delaunay triangulation. knn Named sub-list one entry per k (k3, k5, k7, k10), containing k-NN graph metrics. mst Minimum spanning tree metrics. gabriel Gabriel graph metrics. rng Relative neighborhood graph metrics.

title: Extract graph-based tissue organization features — extract_graph_features
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # nuclei_list is the 'nuc' element of a HoVerNet JSON parsed with jsonToSe feats <- extract_graph_features(nuclei_list, image_dims = c(1024, 1024)) feats$delaunay$transitivity feats$knn$k5$clustering_coefficient } # }

title: Batch import of cell-graph topology files — importAllGraphH5
Batch import of cell-graph topology files — importAllGraphH5
text: fnames Character vector paths .h5 graph files. as_igraph Logical(1). Passed importGraphH5. verbose Logical(1). Print progress messages.

title: Batch import of cell-graph topology files — importAllGraphH5
text: named list (names = TCGA patient barcodes) igraph objects raw lists, depending as_igraph.

title: Batch import of cell-graph topology files — importAllGraphH5
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ h5_files <- list.files(     system.file("extdata", package = "CellGraphR"),     pattern = "_graph\\.h5$",     full.names = TRUE ) graphs <- importAllGraphH5(h5_files) sapply(graphs, igraph::vcount)   # cells per slide } # }

what: Import patient-level graph features as a SummarizedExperiment — importGraphFeatures
title: Import patient-level graph features as a SummarizedExperiment — importGraphFeatures
text: Reads pre-computed patient-level cell-graph feature matrix (CSV) returns SummarizedExperiment ready multi-modal downstream analysis.

title: Import patient-level graph features as a SummarizedExperiment — importGraphFeatures
Import patient-level graph features as a SummarizedExperiment — importGraphFeatures
text: fname Character(1). Path CSV file patient-level graph features. Expected columns: patient_id plus one column per feature. colData data.frame DataFrame additional sample-level annotations (rows must patient IDs). NULL skip. feature_groups Logical(1). TRUE (default), annotate feature thematic group rowData.

title: Import patient-level graph features as a SummarizedExperiment — importGraphFeatures
text: SummarizedExperiment object : assay “graph_features” numeric matrix dimension features \(\times\) patients. colData patient ID plus additional annotations colData. rowData feature name , feature_groups = TRUE, feature_group column classifying feature one 14 thematic groups (see .classify_graph_features).

title: Import patient-level graph features as a SummarizedExperiment — importGraphFeatures
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ csv_file <- system.file(     "extdata", "example_graph_features.csv",     package = "CellGraphR" ) graph_se <- importGraphFeatures(csv_file) dim(graph_se)       # features x patients rowData(graph_se)   # feature annotations with group labels } # }

what: Import cell-graph topology from a PyTables HDF5 file — importGraphH5
title: Import cell-graph topology from a PyTables HDF5 file — importGraphH5
text: Reads nodes edges datasets PyTables-formatted .h5 file produced cell-graph construction pipeline optionally converts result igraph object.

title: Import cell-graph topology from a PyTables HDF5 file — importGraphH5
Import cell-graph topology from a PyTables HDF5 file — importGraphH5
text: fname Character(1). Path .h5 graph file. file must contain two PyTables datasets: nodes (column node_id) edges (columns source target). as_igraph Logical(1). TRUE (default), return igraph object. FALSE, return list nodes edges data frames. patient_id Character(1) NULL. Patient identifier stored graph-level attribute. Inferred TCGA barcode fname NULL.

title: Import cell-graph topology from a PyTables HDF5 file — importGraphH5
text: undirected igraph object (list as_igraph = FALSE) following graph-level attributes: patient_id TCGA patient barcode. source_file Absolute path source file. n_nodes Number cell nodes. n_edges Number edges.

title: Import cell-graph topology from a PyTables HDF5 file — importGraphH5
text: .h5 files use PyTables HDF5 format written pandas.DataFrame.to_hdf(). function reads via reticulate using h5py access raw HDF5 datasets pandas construct data frames — tables (PyTables) back-end required. Ensure Python environment h5py >= 3.0 pandas >= 1.3 available calling function (e.g. pip install h5py pandas).

title: Import cell-graph topology from a PyTables HDF5 file — importGraphH5
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ h5_file <- system.file("extdata", "example_graph.h5", package = "CellGraphR") g <- importGraphH5(h5_file) igraph::vcount(g)   # number of cell nodes igraph::ecount(g)   # number of cell-cell edges igraph::graph_attr(g, "patient_id") } # }

what: Visualize a cell spatial graph — plotGraph
title: Visualize a cell spatial graph — plotGraph
text: Renders igraph object ggplot2 figure.  Nodes positioned spatial coordinates (auto-detected vertex attributes) , coordinate columns found, using Fruchterman-Reingold layout.  Nodes can coloured vertex attribute.

title: Visualize a cell spatial graph — plotGraph
code: plotGraph(   g,   x_col = NULL,   y_col = NULL,   color_by = NULL,   title = NULL,   node_size = 1,   edge_alpha = 0.3,   image = NULL,   image_scale = 1,   wsi_dim = NULL )

title: Visualize a cell spatial graph — plotGraph
text: g undirected igraph object, typically produced importGraphH5 buildSpatialGraph. x_col Character(1) NULL. Vertex attribute name x coordinates.  NULL (default) function probes common names "x", "cx", "centroid_x", "X" order falls back force-directed layout none found. y_col Character(1) NULL. x_col y coordinates ("y", "cy", "centroid_y", "Y"). color_by Character(1) NULL. Vertex attribute used colour nodes.  NULL (default) function probes "cell_type", "type", "label". title Character(1) NULL. Plot title. Defaults patient_id graph attribute available. node_size Numeric(1). Point size passed geom_point. Default 1. edge_alpha Numeric(1) \([0,1]\). Transparency edges. Default 0.3. image Character(1) file path source image (PNG, JPEG, SVS, NDPI, TIFF) pre-loaded raster array (e.g. png::readPNG). provided image rendered background layer beneath graph.  Default NULL (image). image_scale Numeric(1). Scale factor applied vertex coordinates overlaying image.  Use graph coordinates image pixels different resolutions (e.g. 0.5 image half resolution coordinate space).  Default 1 (scaling). wsi_dim Numeric(2) NULL. Width height original whole-slide image pixels, c(width, height).  provided, exact scale factors image_width / wsi_width image_height / wsi_height used map graph coordinates (WSI pixel space) thumbnail image.  gives pixel-accurate alignment recommended image_scale WSI dimensions known.  Default NULL (auto-detect).

title: Visualize a cell spatial graph — plotGraph
text: ggplot object.

title: Visualize a cell spatial graph — plotGraph
code: set.seed(42) nuclei <- lapply(seq_len(40), function(i)     list(centroid = runif(2, 0, 500),          type     = sample(c("neoplastic", "stromal", "immune"), 1)))  g <- buildSpatialGraph(nuclei, type = "delaunay") plotGraph(g)  plotGraph(g, color_by = "type", title = "Delaunay graph")

what: Test factor associations with sample groups — testFactorAssociations
title: Test factor associations with sample groups — testFactorAssociations
text: Tests whether MOFA factor scores significantly associated different sample groups using Kruskal-Wallis test. Grouping variables can specified explicitly selected automatically sample metadata.

title: Test factor associations with sample groups — testFactorAssociations
Test factor associations with sample groups — testFactorAssociations
text: object trained MOFA2 object containing factor scores sample metadata. factor Character integer(1). MOFA factor test (required). groups Character vector. Names metadata columns use grouping variables. NULL (default), function automatically selects columns 2–10 unique non-NA values.

title: Test factor associations with sample groups — testFactorAssociations
text: data.frame one row per tested grouping variable columns: group Name grouping variable. statistic Kruskal-Wallis H statistic. p_value Raw p-value. df Degrees freedom. p_adjusted Benjamini-Hochberg adjusted p-value.

title: Test factor associations with sample groups — testFactorAssociations
text: function: Extracts factor scores specified factor MOFA object. Merges factor scores sample metadata. Selects grouping variables (auto user-specified). Runs Kruskal-Wallis tests applies Benjamini-Hochberg correction. groups = NULL, columns 2–10 unique non-NA values selected (typically represent categorical clinical variables Gleason grade, tumour stage, treatment arm).

title: Test factor associations with sample groups — testFactorAssociations
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Auto-select grouping variables results <- testFactorAssociations(mofa_object, factor = 1)  # Test specific grouping variables results <- testFactorAssociations(mofa_object,                                   factor = "Factor1",                                   groups = c("gleason_grade", "pathologic_stage"))  significant <- results[results$p_adjusted < 0.05, ] } # }
